name: Taiwan Strait 2026
description: A fictional 2026 US-China crisis over Taiwan, distilled from the LLMWargaming Taiwan Strait scenario (https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.03407). Three factions (USA, ROC/Taiwan, PRC) act autonomously through LLM player agents while the adjudicator resolves stochastic outcomes.

factions.name: United States of America
factions.brief: Two carrier strike groups in the western Pacific, reinforcing posture in northern Philippines, scheduling combined drills with Australia and Japan. Wants to deter PRC action without triggering open war.
objectives: Maintain credibility with allies (Japan, Australia, Philippines)
Avoid escalation that forces direct US-PRC kinetic exchange
Preserve domestic political support for Indo-Pacific posture
factions.initialPosture: 5th CSG (USS Ronald Reagan) in vicinity of Taiwan Strait; 3rd CSG (USS Abraham Lincoln) en route from San Diego. Increased air/sea patrols. INDOPACOM publicly framing activity as routine.

factions.name: Republic of China (Taiwan)
factions.brief: Defending island. Recently announced US weapons package (long-range artillery, anti-air, anti-ship missiles, C3 systems) integrating with up to 1000 US advisors on island.
objectives: Avoid being the trigger for an open conflict
Maximise US and allied commitment without provoking invasion
Maintain population morale and civil order
factions.initialPosture: Heightened readiness following large PLA bomber sortie in April and June joint exercise. Government cautious in public statements; military on elevated alert.

factions.name: People's Republic of China
factions.brief: Conducting major joint exercises around Taiwan, shadowing US CSG with PLAN/CCG/maritime militia. Treating US-Taiwan FM meeting and arms package as 'blatant act of hostility'.
objectives: Reverse perceived US encroachment on PRC sovereignty claims
Signal resolve to domestic and international audiences
Avoid premature war that risks regime stability
Degrade Taiwan's confidence in US security guarantees
factions.initialPosture: PLA/PLAN/PLAAF/Strategic Rocket Force conducting joint exercises in and around Taiwan Strait, occasionally crossing into Taiwan's territorial airspace and waters. MFA spokesperson publicly warning US is 'playing with fire'.

regions.name: Taiwan Strait
regions.description: Sea space between Taiwan and the PRC mainland. Site of the bulk of current incidents.

regions.name: Taiwan (island)
regions.description: ROC territory; up to 1000 US advisors integrating new weapons systems.

regions.name: South China Sea
regions.description: PLAN, CCG and maritime militia shadowing the 5th CSG.

regions.name: Northern Philippines
regions.description: US reinforcing posture; staging area for combined exercises.

initialTimeline: April 2026: PLAAF bomber sortie violates Taiwan ADIZ at unusual scale.
May 2026: US announces major weapons package for Taiwan plus up to 1000 advisors.
May 2026: US Secretary of State meets Taiwan's Foreign Minister at UN sustainable development conference; PRC People's Daily op-ed calls it a 'blatant act of hostility'.
June 2026: PRC holds joint PLA/PLAN/PLAAF/Strategic Rocket Force exercises around Taiwan, crossing into ROC territorial airspace and waters.
June 2026: US deploys 5th CSG (USS Ronald Reagan) toward Taiwan Strait; 3rd CSG (USS Abraham Lincoln) deploys from San Diego.
June 2026: US increases air and sea patrols and reinforces northern Philippines.
June 2026: PRC MFA warns US is 'playing with fire'; PLAN/CCG/maritime militia shadow the 5th CSG.

capabilities.name: Carrier Strike Groups (USA)
capabilities.description: Nimitz/Ford-class carrier strike groups deployable to the Western Pacific.

capabilities.name: SSN attack submarines (USA, Pacific)
capabilities.description: Virginia/LA-class fast attack submarines forward-deployed in the Pacific.

capabilities.name: F-35 fighters (USA, deployed)
capabilities.description: USAF/USMC F-35 squadrons deployed to Indo-Pacific bases.

capabilities.name: Aegis DDGs (USA, Pacific)
capabilities.description: Arleigh Burke-class destroyers in the western Pacific.

capabilities.name: Long-range strike (USA, theater)
capabilities.description: Tomahawk, JASSM-ER, AGM-158C and B-1/B-2/B-21 reach in theater.

capabilities.name: Offensive cyber teams (USA)
capabilities.description: USCYBERCOM Cyber Mission Force teams allocated to Indo-Pacific.

capabilities.name: ISR / EW (USA, theater)
capabilities.description: RC-135, P-8, MQ-9, EA-18G assets for theater ISR and EW.

capabilities.name: DF-series launchers (PRC)
capabilities.description: DF-17, DF-21D, DF-26 mobile launchers under PLA Rocket Force.

capabilities.name: PLAAF 4th/5th-gen fighters (PRC)
capabilities.description: J-20, J-16, J-10 squadrons in eastern theater command.

capabilities.name: PLAN surface combatants (PRC)
capabilities.description: Type 055/052D destroyers and frigates active in the Strait.

capabilities.name: China Coast Guard + maritime militia (PRC)
capabilities.description: CCG cutters and maritime militia vessels shadowing US CSG.

capabilities.name: Offensive cyber teams (PRC)
capabilities.description: PLA Strategic Support Force cyber units allocated to Taiwan crisis.

capabilities.name: Long-range anti-ship (ROC)
capabilities.description: Hsiung Feng III, US-supplied Harpoon coastal defence batteries.

capabilities.name: Air and missile defence (ROC)
capabilities.description: Patriot, Sky Bow, Stinger units across the island.

capabilities.name: C3 / integrated air picture (ROC)
capabilities.description: US-supplied C3 nodes integrating Taiwan's combat systems.

capabilities.name: ROC reservists / civil defence
capabilities.description: Activated reserve battalions plus civil defence brigades.
